The people of Ephraim had the same disagreement with Jephthah as they had with Gideon. Pride was the cause of the disagreement; only from it comes conflict. It is wrong to attach names of reproach to people or countries, as is common, especially those who are at a disadvantage. This often causes conflicts that have bad consequences, as it did here. No disagreements are as bitter as those between brothers or rivals for honor. How much we need to watch and pray against negative emotions! May the Lord incline all his people to pursue things which make for peace!

Here is a short account of three more of the judges of Israel. The happiest life for individuals, and the happiest state of society, is one that has few notable events. To live with good reputation and quietness, to be peacefully helpful to those around us, to have a clear conscience; but above all, and without which nothing can help, to enjoy fellowship with God our Savior while we live, and to die at peace with God and man, forms the substance of all that a wise person can desire.
